title: A Simple and Rapid Agarose Gel Electrophoresis Method to Assess CpG Methylation of DNA
author: Marco Paliza-Carre

steps.title: Open TotalLab CLIQS gel analysis software...
steps.instruction: Open TotalLab CLIQS gel analysis software and open the gel image file.

steps.title: Generate a new analysis protocol with the following settings
steps.instruction: Generate a new analysis protocol with the following settings. Check box “Invert Image Intensity” Rolling Ball: 200 Minimum: 1 Noise reduction: 5 %Max Peak: 1 Maximum peak of the: Gel Edges: Automatic Detection

steps.title: On the gel image, select a...
steps.instruction: On the gel image, select a minimal area of interest (AOI) that contains all bands of ladders and samples.

steps.title: Run the protocol you generated above
steps.instruction: Run the protocol you generated above.

steps.title: Confirm that lanes were accurately generated,...
steps.instruction: Confirm that lanes were accurately generated, and bands were accurately defined.

steps.title: Set up Rf calibration
steps.instruction: Set up Rf calibration

steps.title: Open the “Rf Calibration” tab
steps.instruction: Open the “Rf Calibration” tab. Select keep Rf lines aligned with no lane. Deselect “Use curved Rf lines”. Select “Snap Rf lines to Bands when dragging”. Drag the points from the 0 Rf line to the top 20,000 bp band of each ladder. Drag the points from the 1 Rf line to the bottom 75 bp band of each ladder. Delete any extra points that are not snapped to the top or bottom ladder bands and ensure that top Rf line is still labeled 0.000 and bottom Rf line is still labeled 1.000.

steps.title: Select the relevant lane or “all...
steps.instruction: Select the relevant lane or “all lanes” tab at the bottom of the window.

steps.title: Ensure that none of the bands...
steps.instruction: Ensure that none of the bands intended for analysis are oversaturated.

steps.title: Image using gel documentation system with blue light
steps.instruction: Image using gel documentation system with blue light. Use a 400-millisecond exposure time - this may need to be optimized if a different system is used.

steps.title: Cover gel to protect from light...
steps.instruction: Cover gel to protect from light and stain in staining solution 1 hr at 150 rpm on shaker.

steps.title: Make a solution of 1x SYBR...
steps.instruction: Make a solution of 1x SYBR Safe in 150 mL 1x TAE.

steps.title: Run gel at 100V (6
steps.instruction: Run gel at 100V (6.7 volts/centimeter) for 100 minutes.

steps.title: Load 100 ng of sample or...
steps.instruction: Load 100 ng of sample or 300 ng of GeneRuler 1 kb+ ladder per lane. The wells should be 3 mm wide and 1.5 mm thick well. Ensure that ladders flank the sample(s).

steps.title: Prepare 900 mL of 1x TAE running buffer
steps.instruction: Prepare 900 mL of 1x TAE running buffer.

steps.title: Prepare a 0
steps.instruction: Prepare a 0.5 cm thick 1.5% agarose gel.

steps.title: Add 10 µL of 6x TriTrack...
steps.instruction: Add 10 µL of 6x TriTrack DNA loading dye and 3.16 µL of 10% SDS to each reaction.

steps.title: Incubate in 37°C water bath for 1 hr
steps.instruction: Incubate in 37°C water bath for 1 hr.

steps.title: Quick spin in microcentrifuge up to 8000 rpm/6000 g
steps.instruction: Quick spin in microcentrifuge up to 8000 rpm/6000 g.

steps.title: Invert 10 times to mix
steps.instruction: Invert 10 times to mix.

steps.title: Add reagents to 1
steps.instruction: Add reagents to 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tubes as listed below.

steps.title: Digest 1 µg of DNA per 50 µL reaction
steps.instruction: Digest 1 µg of DNA per 50 µL reaction.

steps.title: %s %s
steps.instruction: %s %s

steps.title: Paste into Microsoft Excel and use...
steps.instruction: Paste into Microsoft Excel and use the formula below to calculate mRf, where Rf1 = Rf of band 1, V1 = raw volume of band 1, and n = the number of bands detected in the lane

steps.title: At the top of the window,...
steps.instruction: At the top of the window, select export -> export to clipboard -> measurements table.

steps.title: Determine which bands constitute the intermediatebands...
steps.instruction: Determine which bands constitute the intermediatebands – these lie between the bands produced from digestion offully methylated or fully unmethylated DNA molecules. ForpSV40-sGAD55-BLa, these are bands that fall between 4880 bp and 700 bp, not includingbands at 4880 bp or 700 bp. Then use Microsoft Excel to calculate value forintermediate/total using the formula below.
